SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

A sealed glyph machine transforms any string of exactly 5 glyphs
by one fixed hidden rule.
The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za).
The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.

Probe log (input -> output):
  za-ti-mu-za-lus -> fen-ke-za-fen-ti
  ti-mu-lus-ti-ke -> ke-za-ti-ke-gor
  gor-lus-ti-gor-mu -> mu-ti-ke-mu-za

The machine now reads: ro-za-ti-fen-ti

Write glyph strings dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro.

messages.content:
A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- ploperd: Troashurst (values Flurxiv/Troashurst)
- zeinurm: Clurckan (values Clurckan/Quanush)
- nauven: Broasturck (values Brurnurn/Broasturck)

messages.content:
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. rotate left
2. reverse all
3. every niloash becomes haurmen
4. drop after vlevoax
5. every haurmen becomes heinov

Evidence (input => ORIGINAL wheel output):

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens

rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

messages.content:
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. rotate right
2. drop after husteick
3. every heivol becomes trusteiv
4. swap first last
5. drop before krockoast
6. drop before trirdov
7. rotate right

Evidence (input => ORIGINAL wheel output):
E1: trirdov heivol flanust heivol husteick trirdov flanust husteick => husteick trusteiv flanust trusteiv husteick flanust | shown wheel gives husteick flanust trusteiv flanust trusteiv husteick
E2: trusteiv trusteiv heivol heivol husteick trirdov husteick husteick => husteick trusteiv trusteiv trusteiv husteick husteick | shown wheel gives husteick husteick trusteiv trusteiv trusteiv husteick
E3: heivol husteick trusteiv heivol husteick trusteiv heivol flanust => flanust trusteiv husteick trusteiv husteick trusteiv | shown wheel gives flanust trusteiv trusteiv husteick trusteiv husteick
E4: heivol flanust trirdov heivol krockoast husteick husteick flanust => flanust trusteiv trirdov krockoast husteick | shown wheel gives flanust husteick trusteiv trirdov krockoast

Which rule number was corrupted? (If replacing a different single
rule could also make every evidence tape match, that number is
accepted too.)

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>
